220	/* Bits 10-12 are used for CIST_RECST (see below) */
221	#define CIST_RECST	10
222	#if defined(LUA_COMPAT_LT_LE)
223	#define CIST_LEQ	(1<<13)  /* using __lt for __le */
224	#endif
225	
226	
227	/*
228	** Field CIST_RECST stores the "recover status", used to keep the error
229	** status while closing to-be-closed variables in coroutines, so that
230	** Lua can correctly resume after an yield from a __close method called
231	** because of an error.  (Three bits are enough for error status.)
232	*/
233	#define getcistrecst(ci)     (((ci)->callstatus >> CIST_RECST) & 7)
234	#define setcistrecst(ci,st)  \
235	  check_exp(((st) & 7) == (st),   /* status must fit in three bits */  \
236	            ((ci)->callstatus = ((ci)->callstatus & ~(7 << CIST_RECST))  \
237	                                                  | ((st) << CIST_RECST)))
238	
239	
240	/* active function is a Lua function */
241	#define isLua(ci)	(!((ci)->callstatus & CIST_C))
242	
243	/* call is running Lua code (not a hook) */
244	#define isLuacode(ci)	(!((ci)->callstatus & (CIST_C | CIST_HOOKED)))
245	
246	/* assume that CIST_OAH has offset 0 and that 'v' is strictly 0/1 */
247	#define setoah(st,v)	((st) = ((st) & ~CIST_OAH) | (v))
248	#define getoah(st)	((st) & CIST_OAH)

file.content:
/* Bits 10-12 are used for CIST_RECST (see below) */
#define CIST_RECST	10
#if defined(LUA_COMPAT_LT_LE)
#define CIST_LEQ	(1<<13)  /* using __lt for __le */
#endif


/*
** Field CIST_RECST stores the "recover status", used to keep the error
** status while closing to-be-closed variables in coroutines, so that
** Lua can correctly resume after an yield from a __close method called
** because of an error.  (Three bits are enough for error status.)
*/
#define getcistrecst(ci)     (((ci)->callstatus >> CIST_RECST) & 7)
#define setcistrecst(ci,st)  \
  check_exp(((st) & 7) == (st),   /* status must fit in three bits */  \
            ((ci)->callstatus = ((ci)->callstatus & ~(7 << CIST_RECST))  \
                                                  | ((st) << CIST_RECST)))


/* active function is a Lua function */
#define isLua(ci)	(!((ci)->callstatus & CIST_C))

/* call is running Lua code (not a hook) */
#define isLuacode(ci)	(!((ci)->callstatus & (CIST_C | CIST_HOOKED)))

/* assume that CIST_OAH has offset 0 and that 'v' is strictly 0/1 */
#define setoah(st,v)	((st) = ((st) & ~CIST_OAH) | (v))
#define getoah(st)	((st) & CIST_OAH)


/*
** 'global state', shared by all threads of this state
*/
typedef struct global_State {
  lua_Alloc frealloc;  /* function to reallocate memory */
  void *ud;         /* auxiliary data to 'frealloc' */
  l_mem totalbytes;  /* number of bytes currently allocated - GCdebt */
  l_mem GCdebt;  /* bytes allocated not yet compensated by the collector */
  lu_mem GCestimate;  /* an estimate of the non-garbage memory in use */
  lu_mem lastatomic;  /* see function 'genstep' in file 'lgc.c' */
  stringtable strt;  /* hash table for strings */
  TValue l_registry;
  TValue nilvalue;  /* a nil value */
  unsigned int seed;  /* randomized seed for hashes */
  lu_byte currentwhite;
  lu_byte gcstate;  /* state of garbage collector */
  lu_byte gckind;  /* kind of GC running */
  lu_byte gcstopem;  /* stops emergency collections */
  lu_byte genminormul;  /* control for minor generational collections */
  lu_byte genmajormul;  /* control for major generational collections */
  lu_byte gcstp;  /* control whether GC is running */
  lu_byte gcemergency;  /* true if this is an emergency collection */
  lu_byte gcpause;  /* size of pause between successive GCs */
  lu_byte gcstepmul;  /* GC "speed" */
  lu_byte gcstepsize;  /* (log2 of) GC granularity */
  GCObject *allgc;  /* list of all collectable objects */
  GCObject **sweepgc;  /* current position of sweep in list */
  GCObject *finobj;  /* list of collectable objects with finalizers */
  GCObject *gray;  /* list of gray objects */

971	impl LuaState {
972	    /// Access the process-wide `GlobalState` immutably.
973	    ///
974	    /// C: `G(L)` macro → `state.global()`.
975	    /// macros.tsv: `G → state.global()`
976	    ///
977	    /// PORT NOTE: Returns `std::cell::Ref<GlobalState>` because GlobalState is held in
978	    /// `Rc<RefCell<...>>`. Call sites that do `state.global().field` should work fine
979	    /// via `Deref`. Callers must not hold the `Ref` across a `global_mut()` call.
980	    pub fn global(&self) -> std::cell::Ref<'_, GlobalState> {
981	        self.global.borrow()
982	    }
983	
984	    /// Access the process-wide `GlobalState` mutably.
985	    ///
986	    /// C: `G(L)` + indirect write → `state.global_mut()`.
987	    /// macros.tsv: `G → state.global()` (writes use `state.global_mut()`)
988	    pub fn global_mut(&self) -> std::cell::RefMut<'_, GlobalState> {
989	        self.global.borrow_mut()
990	    }
991	
992	    /// Clone the `Rc` handle to the GlobalState for sharing with a new coroutine.
993	    ///
994	    /// Used in `new_thread` to give the child thread access to the same GlobalState.

## Report

**File / line:** `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs

:1826`

**What `is_main_thread` does:** Returns whether `self` is the main thread (
the originating `LuaState`, as opposed to a coroutine). C: `(G(

L)->mainthread == L)`.

**Implementation rationale:** In Phase B, coroutines are stubbed (design
decision #6) and the main-thread self-referential GcRef cycle is defer
red to Phase D. The `GlobalState.mainthread` slot is left `None` for the main thread itself (see the T
ODO at `state_open`). Therefore `mainthread.is_none()` is the Phase-B-correct discriminator. A
future Phase E coroutine port must replace this with a `GcRef::ptr_eq` against

`global.mainthread`.

**Family members also implemented:** None.

The `is_*` predicate family in this file has ex
actly one other todo'd stub — `CallInfo::is_vararg_func`
at line 311 — which cannot be faithfully implemented from `&self` on `CallInfo` alone: it requires dereferencing `
ci.func` through the runtime stack to fetch the Lua closure and read `proto.is_vararg`, which CallInfo has
no access to. Per task instruction "Skip a sibling if you cannot faithfully implement it — never f

ake," I left it as is.

**Newly-surfaced todos noticed but N

OT implemented (different families):**
- `CallInfo::is_vararg_func` (state.rs:311) — brid

ges through state.stack, not a pure `&self` op.
- `obj_type_name` (state.rs:1827) — type

-name converter; `to_*`/`*_name` family.
- `get_proto_inst
r`, `dump_proto`, `trace_call`, `trace_exec`, `hook_call` (state.rs:1816–1821) — proto/hook families

.
- `CallInfo::recover_status` / `set_recover_status` (state.rs:326–327) — `ci_*` acc

essor family.

**`cargo build -p lua-cli`:** Green. (Finished `dev` profile, 0 errors.)

content.text:
Build is green. Reporting back.

## Report

**File / line:** `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:1826`

**What `is_main_thread` does:** Returns whether `self` is the main thread (the originating `LuaState`, as opposed to a coroutine). C: `(G(L)->mainthread == L)`.

**Implementation rationale:** In Phase B, coroutines are stubbed (design decision #6) and the main-thread self-referential GcRef cycle is deferred to Phase D. The `GlobalState.mainthread` slot is left `None` for the main thread itself (see the TODO at `state_open`). Therefore `mainthread.is_none()` is the Phase-B-correct discriminator. A future Phase E coroutine port must replace this with a `GcRef::ptr_eq` against `global.mainthread`.

**Family members also implemented:** None.

The `is_*` predicate family in this file has exactly one other todo'd stub — `CallInfo::is_vararg_func` at line 311 — which cannot be faithfully implemented from `&self` on `CallInfo` alone: it requires dereferencing `ci.func` through the runtime stack to fetch the Lua closure and read `proto.is_vararg`, which CallInfo has no access to. Per task instruction "Skip a sibling if you cannot faithfully implement it — never fake," I left it as is.

**Newly-surfaced todos noticed but NOT implemented (different families):**
- `CallInfo::is_vararg_func` (state.rs:311) — bridges through state.stack, not a pure `&self` op.
- `obj_type_name` (state.rs:1827) — type-name converter; `to_*`/`*_name` family.
- `get_proto_instr`, `dump_proto`, `trace_call`, `trace_exec`, `hook_call` (state.rs:1816–1821) — proto/hook families.
- `CallInfo::recover_status` / `set_recover_status` (state.rs:326–327) — `ci_*` accessor family.

**`cargo build -p lua-cli`:** Green. (Finished `dev` profile, 0 errors.)
